known_findings.summary: The active chance/protection fee model differs from the current fixed-fee reference.
All three Strike tiers use different critical chance and damage values.
Basic Punch uses item 9690 x20 instead of item 10281 x25.
Vibrancy scroll IDs 51746 and 51466 are registered by Lua but not mapped by XML.
Powerful Featherweight has storage 0 despite the documented Dangerous Depths unlock.
Powerful Vibrancy has storage 0 despite the documented Dream Courts unlock.

assertions: A player without the common Forgotten Knowledge shrine storage cannot open the window.
A player with the common shrine storage can open the window.
A failed gate does not mutate items, gold or storages.
Basic entries are available to free and premium accounts after common shrine access.
Intricate entries require premium account access under the approved target rules.
Powerful entries require premium account access and their family-specific unlock storage.
A hidden entry cannot be applied by sending its numeric runtime ID directly.
The displayed fee and success policy match the approved target model.
Insufficient gold leaves gold, bank balance, materials and slot state unchanged.
Insufficient materials leaves all resources and slot state unchanged.
A successful application consumes each material and gold charge exactly once.
A protected failure follows the approved target model and cannot double-charge.
Each tier exposes the approved critical chance and damage values.
Equipping and unequipping the item adds and removes the effect exactly once.
Save and reload preserve the selected tier and remaining duration.
The shrine lists the approved source chain for every tier.
A successful application consumes the exact source counts.
A rejected application consumes none of the source items.
Each scroll resolves to the corresponding Vibrancy tier.
A valid boot receives the correct imbuement and duration.
The scroll is consumed only after successful application.
An invalid item, occupied Vibrancy category or unavailable slot does not consume the scroll.
An item cannot receive two imbuements from the same category.
The rejection does not consume gold, materials or scrolls.
Other eligible categories remain available in free slots.
Shrine clearing removes only the selected slot and charges exactly 15000 gold.
Insufficient gold leaves the imbuement and resources unchanged.
Etcher clears all active slots and is consumed only on success.
Etcher used on an item without active imbuements is not consumed.
Aggressive imbuements decay while the item is equipped, the player is in combat and outside protection zones.
They do not decay while the player is out of combat, in a protection zone or while the item is inside a backpack.
Crossing a decay boundary cannot remove stats more than once.
Non-aggressive imbuements decay while their item is directly equipped.
They do not decay while their item is stored in a container.
The optional non-aggressive-fight-only configuration changes behavior exactly as documented.
Imbuement IDs, slots and remaining duration survive save, logout and login.
An expired effect is absent after reload and its modifiers are not applied.
Repeated equip or login paths do not duplicate modifiers.
Only categories and tiers declared by the item metadata appear in the shrine.
Items with no imbuement slots are rejected.
Native conflicting bonuses are not offered under the approved rules.
Leg items are not imbuable under the current referenced rules.
The complete active item set is compared with the current reference as a separate deterministic audit.
